summary:
\boxed{The highest caries susceptibility for primary teeth follows a specific progression based primarily on their late emergence relative to other types—second molars are most susceptible followed closely by first molars then canines due to delayed formation and less time under complete enamel coverage during early childhood years when dietary habits often promote tooth decay more readily.}

This summary captures both critical aspects—their chronological appearance affecting overall dental health—and highlights why secondary molars rank as having increased risks over others among primary dentition stages.
